threat_model: Command payload redaction must preserve governed metadata references needed for receipt correlation without preserving secret-derived strings. A broad metadata hash bypass could leak credential hashes or token-derived identifiers. The mitigation is an explicit metadata reference allowlist plus compound secret-name detection before any reference preservation is considered.

required_checks.check: redaction test proves api_key, api_key_hash, password_hash, and nested intent api keys are masked
receipt integrity check proves goal_intake_preview_id and source_sender_id_hash metadata references remain stable
audit visibility check covers gateway command redaction behavior in the focused command-spine regression suite

findings.mitigation: replace broad metadata *_hash preservation with explicit reference-hash allowlist and compound secret field detection

mitigations: detect compound secret field names before preserving text
preserve only named metadata reference identifiers
preserve only explicitly allowed metadata reference hashes
cover secret-like hash fields and allowed planning references in the same regression test
